context_bundle_source: Read the actual commit diff (git show), agents/llm/factory.py, agents/llm/ollama_client.py, data/synthetic_corpus/topics.yaml, and ran tests directly. Did not read implementer's plan/reasoning artifacts, per isolation mandate (invariant I4).
ollama_only_guarantee_independent_conclu: CONFIRMED SOUND, no gap found. Verified by direct code read (not trusting the commit message): (1) grep of data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py and data/test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py for 'openrouter'/'gemini'/'OPENROUTER_API_KEY'/'GEMINI_API_KEY' finds zero code-level usage -- only prose in docstrings/comments explaining why they are excluded, and a static guard test (test_no_openrouter_or_gemini_code_references_in_source) that asserts this at the source-text level. (2) generate_corpus's default client path calls create_llm_client(provider='ollama') with a hardcoded string literal (module constant _REQUIRED_PROVIDER = 'ollama'). (3) Read agents/llm/factory.py directly: create_llm_client resolves `provider if provider is not None else os.environ.get(PROVIDER_ENV_VAR)` -- when provider is passed explicitly (as this module always does), the LLM_PROVIDER env var is never even consulted, so a future .env setting LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter/gemini cannot affect this script's default path. Confirmed empirically: test_generate_corpus_default_client_uses_ollama_provider_explicitly sets LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter via monkeypatch and asserts create_llm_client is still called with provider='ollama'; re-ran it independently and it passed. (4) Read agents/llm/ollama_client.py directly to check whether OllamaClient itself reads any environment variable that could redirect it to a remote/non-local endpoint: it does not -- base_url defaults to the hardcoded literal 'http://localhost:11434' via a constructor default, with zero os.environ calls anywhere in the file. The only way this script would ever talk to a non-local/non-Ollama endpoint is if a caller explicitly passed base_url= or a whole different llm_client= into generate_corpus -- which is an explicit, visible caller action, not an ambient env-var/config surprise. This matches the letter and spirit of the standing user preference in .cdr/memory/pending.md. (5) Confirmed no .env file exists anywhere in the repo (find -iname '.env*' returned nothing) and neither new file references .env. No gap found in the Ollama-only guarantee.

dimensions.requirements_conformance: PASS. Acceptance criteria (generate synthetic corpus of policy/manual PDFs seeded with predefined topics + deliberate cross-topic references) is met: topics.yaml seeds 10 topics (id/title/key_facts), build_doc_specs deterministically assigns each topic as one doc's primary subject plus 2 distinct cross-referenced topics (verified no self-reference, verified distinct via read + passing tests), build_prompt explicitly instructs the LLM to name-check each cross-referenced topic. OQ-1 resolution (LLM-authored via local Ollama only, rendered via lightweight library) is met and independently verified (see ollama_only_guarantee_independent_conclusion). Test spec ('implementer's judgment call, verify N PDFs generated, topic + cross-topic reference presence, PDF validity') is satisfied by test_generate_corpus_produces_expected_pdf_count, test_generate_corpus_pdfs_are_valid_and_parseable (uses fitz to reopen and parse), and test_generate_corpus_pdf_text_contains_primary_and_cross_topic.
dimensions.architecture_conformance: PASS. Follows the established data/ <-> agents/ cross-root import precedent from agents/eval/datasets.py (5.1.1) via _ensure_cross_root_imports/sys.path insertion, mirrored in the opposite direction. Uses the existing agents/llm/factory.create_llm_client dispatch layer rather than importing OllamaClient directly, consistent with docs/LLD's 'selection via config, not call-site code changes' rule for every other call site -- while correctly special-casing this one call site's need to pin provider explicitly, which is disclosed and justified in the module docstring, not a silent architecture violation.
dimensions.regression_risk: PASS. git show --stat confirms exactly 3 new files (data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py +440, data/synthetic_corpus/topics.yaml +81, data/test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py +340), zero changes to agents/llm/*, agents/eval/datasets.py, or any existing data/ loader. Independently ran the full data/ suite (34 passed: 14 pre-existing + 20 new) confirming no regressions in adjacent loaders (load_bitext.py, load_enron.py, test_loaders.py all still pass).
dimensions.edge_cases: CHANGES NOTED (non-blocking). Found one real, independently-verified defect via direct source read: render_pdf's page-overflow-handling loop (data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py, render_pdf, ~lines 298-320) claims in its docstring to 'add pages as needed if the body text overflows a single page ... looped here until all text fits, or a reasonable page-count safety cap is hit' and to raise RuntimeError if text still does not fit after max_pages=20. In actual code, the for-loop body unconditionally executes a second insert_textbox() attempt at 85% font size on overflow and then sets remaining='' and break unconditionally -- it never checks whether that second, smaller-font attempt itself still overflows, and it always exits the loop after exactly one iteration regardless. Consequences: (a) max_pages/the multi-page mechanism is dead code, a second page is never actually created for any document, contradicting the docstring; (b) the documented RuntimeError safety net for text that still does not fit is unreachable in the overflow branch, since remaining is unconditionally cleared before the post-loop check -- so an LLM completion long enough to overflow even the smaller font would be silently truncated with no error, no warning, and no test coverage (grep of test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py confirms no test exercises insert_textbox/overflow/max_pages/render_pdf directly). Not currently triggered in practice (all sampled/live 3-5-paragraph completions fit on one page, confirmed via passing tests and the live-Ollama smoke test), so non-blocking for this subtask's own acceptance criteria, but a real, disclosed-as-absent-but-actually-present robustness gap worth fixing before this generator is scaled to longer completions or a larger topic set.
dimensions.security: PASS. No new external-input trust boundary of concern: LLM completion text is only ever written into a PDF text box (fitz insert_textbox), never eval'd/exec'd/interpreted as markup or code. No new secrets, no new network surface beyond the existing OllamaClient's already-reviewed HTTP path. yaml.safe_load (not yaml.load) is used for topics.yaml, avoiding arbitrary object deserialization.
dimensions.performance: PASS, not a concern for this subtask's scope. Corpus generation is an offline batch script (not a request-path component); topics.yaml scaling to 30-50 entries multiplies LLM calls and file I/O linearly, acceptable for an offline data-generation tool.
dimensions.maintainability: PASS. Module docstring is unusually thorough and explicitly discloses every non-obvious design decision (Ollama-only rationale, fitz-over-reportlab/weasyprint deviation, manifest.json schema intent for 5.1.3) which materially aided independent verification. One overclaim found: render_pdf's docstring describes multi-page/overflow handling that the code does not actually implement as described (see edge_cases finding) -- a documentation/implementation mismatch, not a functional regression, but should be corrected.
dimensions.test_coverage: PASS. Independently re-ran (not trusted from the commit message): cd data && agents/.venv/bin/python -m pytest test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py -v -> 20 passed (confirmed genuine, not trivially-true: includes topic-manifest validation error paths, deterministic doc-spec assignment incl. no-self-reference and distinctness checks, PDF validity via real fitz re-parse, manifest.json shape, the env-var-ignore test, and the static no-OpenRouter/Gemini-references guard). Full data/ suite: 34 passed (14 pre-existing + 20 new), confirming zero regression in sibling loaders. Did not re-run the full agents/ suite (348 tests, ~4min) given zero files under agents/ were touched by this commit (confirmed via git show --stat) -- regression risk there is definitionally nil for this diff. Live-Ollama smoke test independently confirmed to have actually executed (not skipped): curl localhost:11434/api/tags confirmed Ollama reachable with llama3.1:8b present at verification time, and the smoke test's 30s wall-clock contribution to the 20-test run is consistent with a real model call (an httpx.get-unreachable-server skip would be near-instant). Coverage gap: no test exercises render_pdf's overflow/multi-page path (see edge_cases finding).
dimensions.confidence: HIGH. All claims in the commit message were independently re-derived from source and live execution rather than trusted: exact file/line diff stat, hardcoded-provider mechanism (verified by reading factory.py's actual resolution order, not just the test), OllamaClient's lack of any redirecting env var, live test re-run with matching pass counts, live Ollama reachability, and topics.yaml's actual topic count and non-hardcoded scaling behavior (build_doc_specs takes topics as a runtime dict, no length-10 assumption anywhere in the code).

specific_checks.2_no_env_file: CONFIRMED. `find . -iname '.env*'` repo-wide returns nothing; new files contain zero `.env` reads (only prose mentions in comments explaining why none is needed).
specific_checks.3_test_coverage_genuineness: CONFIRMED via independent re-run: 20/20 in data/test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py, 34/34 in data/ (matches claim). Did not independently re-run the full 348-test agents/ suite (unnecessary given zero agents/ files touched) -- this is a minor scope limitation of this verification, not a disproof of the claim.
specific_checks.4_manifest_schema_soundness: CONFIRMED sound for 5.1.3's stated purpose: manifest.json's documents[] entries carry doc_id, filename, primary_topic{id,title}, and cross_references[{id,title}] -- topic ids (not just titles) are present, letting 5.1.3 build ground truth keyed on stable ids rather than re-parsing PDF text or fuzzy-matching titles.
specific_checks.5_regression_risk_file_scope: CONFIRMED via git show --stat: exactly the 3 claimed files, no changes to agents/ or existing data/ loaders.
specific_checks.6_fitz_pdf_creation_validity: CONFIRMED fitz (pymupdf 1.28.0 / MuPDF 1.29.0, already agents/pyproject.toml-pinned >=1.24) genuinely supports PDF creation via fitz.open() with no args + new_page() + insert_textbox() + save(), independently verified both by direct code read and by the passing test_generate_corpus_pdfs_are_valid_and_parseable test which reopens generated PDFs with a fresh fitz.open(path) call. Reasonable, disclosed substitution for OQ-1's 'e.g.' example libraries -- but see edge_cases finding for a real defect in the overflow-handling wrapper around this otherwise-sound library usage.
specific_checks.7_topic_seed_scale_generalization: CONFIRMED. topics.yaml ships 10 topics (independently counted via yaml.safe_load, not just grep). build_doc_specs/load_topics take topics as a runtime-loaded dict with no hardcoded length assumption anywhere in gen_synthetic_pdfs.py; cross_refs_per_doc is clamped via `min(cross_refs_per_doc, n-1)` generically. Confirmed the code would generalize correctly to 30-50 entries with zero code changes, matching the commit message's own disclosed claim.

non_blocking_findings.title: render_pdf's documented multi-page overflow handling is dead code; text can silently truncate with no error, contradicting its own docstring
non_blocking_findings.evidence: data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py render_pdf(): the for-loop's overflow branch always executes a second insert_textbox() at 85% font size then unconditionally sets remaining='' and break, regardless of whether the second attempt itself overflowed. max_pages is therefore unreachable/dead, and the documented RuntimeError safety net is unreachable in the overflow branch. No test exercises this path (grep of test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py confirms zero references to insert_textbox/overflow/max_pages).
non_blocking_findings.recommendation: Check the second insert_textbox()'s return value; only clear remaining and break if it is >= 0. If it still overflows, either genuinely continue the page loop (create doc.new_page() again with the still-overflowing remainder) or raise the already-documented RuntimeError instead of silently truncating. Add a regression test using an artificially long fake-LLM completion to exercise the overflow path.

recommendation: PASS_WITH_COMMENTS. The subtask's core, elevated-scrutiny requirement -- Ollama-only, never OpenRouter/Gemini, no .env -- was independently re-derived from source (not trusted from the commit message) across all four layers checked (source grep, hardcoded-literal call, factory.py's actual env-var resolution order, OllamaClient's own lack of any redirecting env var) and found genuinely sound with no gap. All other acceptance criteria, architecture-conformance expectations, and test-coverage claims were independently re-verified and confirmed. One real, non-blocking documentation/implementation-mismatch defect was found in render_pdf's overflow handling (dead multi-page code path, unreachable documented RuntimeError, silent-truncation risk for long completions) -- recommended for a follow-up fix before this generator is scaled to longer documents or the full 30-50 topic set, but does not block this subtask's own acceptance criteria or the Ollama-only guarantee.
